page_content:
TLPL743 Teaching Multilingual Learners: Current and Future Research Directions in Applied Linguistics and Language Education (3 Credits) Examines the research base on teacher education through an equity- oriented lens, asking the larger question: How can we prepare teachers of multilingual learners to engage in humanizing and asset-oriented pedagogies? It also explores opportunities for future research in teacher education. Credit Only Granted for: EDCI734 or TLPL743. Formerly: EDCI734.

TLPL744 Research Centering Multilingual Learners: Sociocultural Approaches to Applied Linguistics in Education (3 Credits) Critically examine research relevant to multilingual students, bilingual education, and theories of second language acquisition and multilingual development. Students will apply sociocultural perspectives to understand educational contexts and practices that can lead to greater educational equity for culturally and linguistically diverse students.

TLPL745 Classroom Inquiry Toward Teacher Professional Development (2 Credits) Emphasizes current research and best practice on teacher inquiry as a mode of professional development. Participants will work within a teacher-coaching model to create a professional development presentation on writing and classroom writing instruction.
